title: Appendix: Career Timeline
author: Peter S. Kastner
abstract: A structured career timeline spanning Kastner's six-decade arc in computing, from 1960 bookkeeping frustrations through mainframe operation, consulting, vendor marketing, and co-founding Aberdeen Group. The appendix also documents key technology platform transitions witnessed first-hand, Aberdeen Group's complete financial history (1990–2006), and a detailed founding chronology of the Transaction Processing Performance Council (TPC).
importance_rationale: Provides the definitive factual chronology of Kastner's career with named employers, roles, dates, and quantitative financial data not consolidated anywhere else in the memoir.
relevance_rationale: Every major entity, technology transition, and organizational inflection point in the memoir is anchored here with precise dates and metrics.
prescience_rationale: The 1987 ghostwritten Yankee Group paper predicting microprocessor OLTP dominance and the TPC co-founding demonstrate forward-looking analytical judgment that proved correct.
